run_metadata.coverage_summary: Queried all nine Tier-1 government jurisdictions (AusTender, buy.nsw, Buying for Victoria, QTenders/supply.qld, Tenders WA/SA/Tasmania, NT QTOL, ACT Tenders) via web search; sampled Tier-2 water corporations (Sydney/Melbourne/SA Water, Power & Water NT), councils (VendorPanel public tenders, Inner West) and universities; and covered Tier-3 corporate channels (SAP Ariba Discovery, BHP, Rio Tinto Buy Local, Fortescue, NBN Co, Australia Post, Snowy Hydro). Captured one genuinely new opportunity (UNSW enterprise environmental monitoring EOI) and two new buyer-originated sources (Power & Water NT opportunities page; UNSW E-Tendering portal). As in recent runs, direct page fetching was unavailable (every external portal returned HTTP 403 to the fetch tool), so detail is limited to publicly-searchable headline level and no corporate RFx could be read behind registration walls.
gaps: Direct page retrieval (WebFetch) returned HTTP 403 for every external site this run — including tenders.gov.au ATM index and detail pages, buy.nsw, Tenders WA/SA/Tasmania, NT QTOL, ACT Tenders, Sydney Water, Rio Tinto Buy Local and Inner West Council. Tender detail pages and specification documents could not be opened, so deliverables/requirements extraction was not possible; findings rely on public search snippets only.

AusTender (tenders.gov.au) ATM list and individual ATM detail pages were reachable only via the search index, not direct fetch; specific publish/close dates for individual current ATMs could not be confirmed from the notice itself.

Tier-3 corporate opportunities (SAP Ariba Discovery, BHP Find Opportunities, Rio Tinto Ariba/Buy Local, NBN Co via Ariba, Fortescue) are login/registration-gated; no publicly-visible corporate RFx was found. Portals are recorded in discovered_sources with access_method=portal-registration; no corporate tenders were fabricated.
Invitation-only / panel-restricted RFx (e.g. management-consultancy RFPs) are out of scope and were not sought.

Web search index appears to lag by roughly 1-2 weeks: several returned listings had closing dates already in late June / early-to-mid July 2026 (already closed relative to the run date) and were excluded rather than recorded as open.

Power and Water Corporation (NT) QTOL showed a tender closing 23/07/2026 14:00 ACST, but the title and reference could not be resolved without portal access; the portal is recorded as a new source but the individual tender was not captured (no fabrication).
UNSW EOI closing date (2026-07-27) is taken from aggregated search listings; the buyer's own detail page sits behind the UNSW TenderLink login and could not be independently verified this run.
recommended_seed_additions: Power and Water Corporation (NT) — Opportunities to tender (https://www.powerwater.com.au/about/working-with-us/contractors-and-suppliers/opportunities-to-tender) — GBE, NT, buyer-originated.
University of New South Wales — E-Tendering portal via TenderLink (https://portal.tenderlink.com/unsw/) — university, NSW, free-registration buyer portal.

tenders.title: EOI for Enterprise Environmental Monitoring System for Cold Storage and Laboratory Infrastructure
tenders.description: UNSW is seeking an enterprise-level environmental monitoring system to support critical cold storage and laboratory infrastructure across multiple locations on the Kensington campus.
tenders.delivery_location: UNSW Kensington campus, Sydney NSW (multiple locations)
tenders.reasoning: Discovered via web search of Australian university procurement; UNSW publishes its own tenders through the buyer-originated UNSW E-Tendering portal (portal.tenderlink.com/unsw). An aggregator listing (australiantenders.com.au id 594385) was used only as a discovery signal, not as the source of truth.
tenders.notes: Headline-level only. Closing date 2026-07-27 is reported in aggregated search listings; the buyer's full detail page and any documents are behind the UNSW TenderLink free-registration login and could not be opened this run (fetch returned HTTP 403), so deliverables, requirements, evaluation criteria, value and contacts remain unverified and pending a detail pass.

discovered_sources.source_name: Power and Water Corporation (NT) — Opportunities to tender
discovered_sources.how_discovered: Search for NT government-owned utility procurement; Power & Water Corporation advertises current tenders on its own supplier pages and via NT QTOL. A live tender closing 23/07/2026 14:00 ACST was observed on QTOL but its title/reference could not be resolved without portal access.
discovered_sources.notes: Government-owned corporation (electricity, water, sewerage) for the Northern Territory; buyer-originated. Larger works also appear on tendersonline.nt.gov.au (QTOL).

discovered_sources.source_name: University of New South Wales — E-Tendering (TenderLink)
discovered_sources.how_discovered: Found while searching Australian university tenders; UNSW runs its own buyer-originated e-tendering portal on TenderLink (free supplier registration) where EOIs/RFTs such as the Enterprise Environmental Monitoring System EOI are lodged.
discovered_sources.notes: Buyer-originated university portal (not an aggregator). Full opportunity detail and documents require free supplier registration.

discovered_sources.how_discovered: Canonical Tier-1 seed; checked this run (current ATM list) via the search index. Direct fetch returned HTTP 403.
discovered_sources.notes: Central Commonwealth procurement portal. Individual ATM detail pages could not be fetched directly this run.

discovered_sources.notes: NSW Government Opportunities Hub. Several known open tenders (natural gas to public hospitals, koala monitoring, Bradfield Bus Interchange EOI) remain listed.

discovered_sources.how_discovered: Canonical Tier-1 seed; checked this run via search. No specific open ACT opportunity surfaced in search snippets this run.
Canonical Tier-2 seed; checked this run. Most councils publish here; several council RFTs surfaced (Inner West, Wujal Wujal) though with closing dates already passed relative to the run date.
discovered_sources.notes: Buyer-originated platform for councils, GOCs, NFPs and universities.

discovered_sources.how_discovered: Mandatory Tier-3 check each run. Public posting board for corporate RFx; used by NBN Co, Rio Tinto, BHP (Enterprise), Coles and others. No publicly-visible Australian RFx was retrievable without a supplier account this run.
discovered_sources.notes: Largest single slice of corporate opportunity; full detail requires free supplier registration.
